evidence.method: Artifact-bound review of the rendered HTML, 1440/390/320 captures, geometry, overflow, fonts, console and request-failure receipt.
limitations: Expert proxy and deterministic runtime evidence are not eye tracking or participant research.
Blind target-user alignment was proven on the semantic sketch; the finished system still requires a fresh Human-QA pass.

intent.user_goal: Understand whether VibeUs offers a public place for an AI-assisted project and inspect examples before registering.
intent.business_goal: Send a qualified cold visitor to the public project catalog before asking for authentication.
intent.decision_moment: The visitor decides whether the promise is concrete enough to inspect the live catalog.
intent.desired_action: Open the current public catalog without registration.

predicted_route.label: Социальная платформа для вайбкодеров
predicted_route.semantic_job: Name the product category and audience before the promise.
predicted_route.attention_receipt: Upper-left placement and coral rule precede the display message without competing with it.

predicted_route.label: Дайте проекту публичную жизнь
predicted_route.semantic_job: State the concrete visitor change in the dominant reading mass.
predicted_route.attention_receipt: Largest type, strongest coral field and primary upper-left axis dominate both desktop and mobile captures.

predicted_route.label: Fresh VibeUs public record
predicted_route.semantic_job: Turn the promise into an inspectable owned product object.
predicted_route.attention_receipt: Readable product screenshot, literal route bar, capture date and connector line form one bounded proof object.

predicted_route.label: Смотреть проекты
predicted_route.semantic_job: Offer the public-before-auth destination after category, outcome and proof.
predicted_route.attention_receipt: Filled coral rectangle with black action text is isolated beneath the lead and remains fully visible at 1440, 390 and 320 widths.

zones.label: Outcome message
zones.role: Concentrates the promise in the first upper-left reading axis.

zones.label: Owned record proof
zones.role: Makes the promise inspectable without becoming fictional spectacle.

zones.label: Primary catalog action
zones.role: Converts understanding into a low-commitment public route.

zones.label: Catalog-before-auth condition
zones.role: Reduces registration risk adjacent to the action.

axes_grid.note: Desktop uses a 12-column split; mobile collapses to one authored axis without DOM-order inversion.

alignment.note: Category, headline, lead, actions and condition share one left anchor; proof metadata and screenshot share a separate bounded frame.

spacing.note: Local 18–30px grouping distinguishes related copy; the 38px mobile proof gap marks the message-to-evidence transition.

grouping.note: Action consequence is adjacent to the CTA; capture date, URL, screenshot and evidence labels remain one proof object.

typography.note: Onest display establishes one dominant message; body and operational text remain at readable sizes including 320px.

affordance.note: Primary and secondary actions are visually distinct, literal and destination-bound.

fold.note: Desktop shows the full contract; 390 and 320 show category, outcome, CTA, risk condition and the beginning of the labelled proof object.

density_rhythm.note: Dense product proof follows a concise message field; the second chapter reverses to a calm editorial explanation and large catalog object.

whitespace.note: Open space separates message and proof axes and never leaves a screen without value, action or inspectable evidence.

responsive.note: 1440, 390 and exact 320 captures have loaded fonts, zero document overflow and complete primary action visibility.

channel_stacking: Outcome: size + coral color + upper-left position
Proof: image detail + depth + literal route

findings.evidence: The first viewport binds the dominant promise to a fresh owned record and exposes the direct public action at every required width.
findings.user_impact: A cold visitor can understand, verify and act without first interpreting abstract spectacle.
findings.fix: Preserve this message-proof-action contract during system build.
findings.acceptance: Final captures retain readable product proof, direct catalog route and no competing primary CTA.

findings.evidence: The expired fixture suppresses the catalog screenshot and shows a literal live-route recovery instead of stale success.
findings.user_impact: The visitor is not misled when volatile proof is no longer current.
findings.fix: Reuse the same degraded-state contract in the final proof chapter.
findings.acceptance: Expired and error fixtures expose no stale screenshot, count or success wording.
